title: Computer Science Hardcore Challenge
description: Test your advanced computer science knowledge with 15 tough questions covering algorithms, data structures, machine learning, and more. You have only 5 minutes (remainTime = 300) to prove your expertise!

questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of inserting an element into a Red-Black Tree in the worst case?
questions.explanation: Red-Black Trees maintain balance, so insertions take O(log n) time in the worst case.

questions.questionText: Which algorithm is commonly used to find the shortest path in a graph with non-negative edge weights?
questions.explanation: Dijkstra’s algorithm efficiently finds the shortest path in graphs with non-negative weights.

questions.questionText: Which of the following techniques reduces overfitting in deep learning models?
questions.explanation: Dropout randomly disables neurons during training to reduce overfitting.

questions.questionText: Which data structure is best suited for implementing a LRU (Least Recently Used) cache?
options: C) HashMap with Doubly Linked List
questions.explanation: An LRU cache can be efficiently implemented using a HashMap and a Doubly Linked List for O(1) operations.

questions.questionText: What is the space complexity of a recursive DFS traversal on a graph?
questions.explanation: Recursive DFS uses the call stack, which may grow up to O(V) in depth for a connected graph.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is most effective against SQL injection?
questions.explanation: Parameterized queries prevent attackers from injecting malicious SQL code.

questions.questionText: Which protocol is used for secure communication over the Internet?
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses SSL/TLS to encrypt HTTP data, ensuring secure communication.

questions.questionText: What is the vanishing gradient problem?
options: B) Gradients become too small during backpropagation
questions.explanation: In deep networks, gradients can become very small, preventing effective learning.

questions.questionText: In software design, what does the term 'coupling' refer to?
options: B) Degree of interdependence between modules
C) Number of methods in a class
questions.explanation: Coupling measures how closely connected different modules or classes are.

questions.questionText: What is the main advantage of using indexes in a database?
questions.explanation: Indexes allow quick data retrieval, significantly improving query performance.

questions.questionText: What is a foreign key in relational databases?
options: A) Primary identifier of a record
B) A key used to sort records
C) A key referencing a primary key in another table
questions.explanation: A foreign key ensures referential integrity between tables.

questions.questionText: Which type of learning uses labeled data?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning uses labeled datasets to train models to make predictions.

questions.questionText: Which loss function is commonly used for binary classification?
questions.explanation: Cross-entropy measures the performance of a classification model whose output is a probability.

questions.questionText: What does a digital signature ensure?
questions.explanation: Digital signatures verify that the message has not been tampered with and comes from a valid sender.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a symmetric encryption algorithm?
questions.explanation: AES is a widely-used symmetric key encryption algorithm.

rewards.description: Earn 1500 points for completing the Computer Science Hardcore Challenge!
